framing: Multi-agent orchestration patterns that complement traditional cloud design patterns for AI workloads. Microsoft recommends starting at the lowest level of complexity: Direct model call → Single agent with tools → Multi-agent orchestration.
complexity_levels: Direct model call — single LLM call with a well-crafted prompt; no agent logic.
Single agent with tools — one agent reasons over available tools and knowledge sources in a loop. Often the right default for enterprise use cases.
Multi-agent orchestration — multiple specialized agents that coordinate via an orchestrator or peer protocol. Justified when a single agent can't reliably handle the task.

patterns.name: Sequential orchestration
patterns.summary: Agents are chained in a predefined linear order. Each agent processes the previous agent's output. Routing is deterministic.
patterns.when_to_use: Multi-stage processes with clear linear dependencies; data transformation pipelines; progressive refinement (draft → review → polish).
patterns.watch_out_for: Failures in early stages propagate; no parallelism.

patterns.name: Concurrent orchestration
patterns.summary: Multiple agents run simultaneously on the same input. Each provides independent analysis from a unique specialization. Results may be aggregated (vote, weighted merge, LLM-synthesized summary) or applied independently.
patterns.when_to_use: Independent analysis from multiple perspectives; latency-sensitive scenarios; brainstorming, ensemble reasoning, quorum/voting decisions.
patterns.watch_out_for: Requires conflict-resolution when results contradict; resource-intensive.

patterns.name: Group chat orchestration
patterns.summary: Multiple agents collaborate in a shared conversation thread. A chat manager controls turn order. Supports human-in-the-loop participation. Includes the sub-pattern Maker-Checker loops (also known as evaluator-optimizer, generator-verifier, critic loops, reflection loops).
patterns.when_to_use: Brainstorming; decision-making via debate and consensus; quality-assurance with structured review; compliance validation requiring multiple expert perspectives.
patterns.watch_out_for: Conversation loops; control degrades with many agents — Microsoft recommends ≤ 3 agents.

patterns.name: Handoff orchestration
patterns.summary: Dynamic delegation between specialized agents. Each agent can assess the task and decide to handle it or transfer control to a more appropriate agent. Only one agent is active at a time; full control transfers on each handoff.
patterns.when_to_use: The optimal agent isn't known upfront and requirements emerge during processing; multi-domain problems with one specialist active at a time.
patterns.watch_out_for: Infinite handoff loops; unpredictable routing paths.

patterns.name: Magentic orchestration
patterns.summary: Designed for open-ended problems with no predetermined plan. A manager agent dynamically builds and refines a task ledger of goals/subgoals through collaboration with specialized agents that use tools to change external systems. Iterates, backtracks, and re-plans as context evolves.
patterns.when_to_use: Open-ended problems without a predetermined solution path; need for an auditable plan produced before/after execution.
patterns.watch_out_for: Slow to converge; stalls on ambiguous goals; cost variance is high.

implementation_considerations_from_sourc: Context and state management: monitor accumulated context size; compact (summarize/prune) between agents; persist long-running state externally.
Reliability: timeouts, retries, circuit breakers, validate agent output before passing on, isolate agents from shared single points of failure.
Security: authn/secure networking between agents, least privilege, user-identity propagation with security trimming, content-safety guardrails at multiple points.
Cost: match model size to agent task; monitor token consumption per agent; compact context between agents.
Observability and testing: instrument all agent operations and handoffs; use LLM-as-judge / scoring rubrics rather than exact-match assertions.
Human-in-the-loop: scope HITL gates to specific tool invocations; persist state at checkpoints.
implementations_called_out_by_source: Semantic Kernel (migration path to Agent Framework)
